intro: Anthropic closed a $65 billion Series H funding round at a $965 billion valuation, positioning the AI company for a potential IPO as one of the sector's most valuable private firms. Robinhood Markets launched beta products allowing AI agents to autonomously execute stock trades and manage credit card spending on its platform. Mistral unveiled Mistral for Industrial Engineering with Airbus, BMW, and EDF as initial customers, advancing its strategy for AI applications in physical manufacturing and infrastructure sectors.

whitelist.title: AI - Business Insider
whitelist.desc: Explore cutting-edge AI insights and trends with Business Insider's dedicated artificial intelligence coverage. Stay ahead with in-depth articles, expert analyses, and the latest news on AI technologies shaping industries worldwide.

whitelist.title: Boston Seizes on California Billionaire Tax to Lure AI Talent
whitelist.desc: The talent at some of America’s hottest artificial intelligence companies often passes through Boston-area universities before heading west to build billion-dollar businesses in Silicon Valley. Massachusetts business and political leaders say California’s proposed tax on billionaires is an opportunity to change that.

whitelist.title: Anthropic raises $65 Billion, nears $1T valuation ahead of IPO | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Anthropic has closed a $65 billion Series H round at a $965 billion post-money valuation, marking what could be the AI startup's final private fundraise before a highly anticipated IPO.

whitelist.title: Artificial intelligence: Latest News and Updates | South China Morning Post
whitelist.desc: News articles, analyses and commentary about machines that mimic human cognitive functions such as learning and problem solving. This also covers chatbots, algorithms, consumer products and Big Data analysis that make use of AI.

whitelist.title: “Don’t use AI just for the sake of using AI.”
whitelist.desc: That’s what Amazon exec Dave Treadwell told employees after the company shuttered an internal leaderboard tracking which staff members use AI the most, the Financial Times reports: > The decision came after the tool led some workers to assign AI agents — autonomous bots that can take actions on behalf of users — to carry out needless tasks in an apparent attempt to climb the rankings. As computing becomes more expensive, employee “toxenmaxxing” reportedly increased costs at Amazon.

whitelist.title: Starbucks quietly retired its AI agent just months after deployment after it hallucinated coffee shop inventories and slowed down baristas | Fortune
whitelist.desc: “It started off not particularly accurate and got less accurate over time,” one Starbucks employee told Fortune.

whitelist.title: Tokenmaxxing is dead. It didn't produce the AI ROI companies wanted. | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Token usage is a poor proxy for company-wide productivity gains that only come from redesigning workflows, not just automating them.

whitelist.title: Illinois is close to enacting an AI safety law with broader mandates than other states’.
whitelist.desc: Governor JB Pritzker says he plans to sign a bill passed Wednesday by the state legislature, which would require independent audits and whistleblower protections at AI companies. Those features go beyond recently passed AI safety laws in New York and California, according to NBC News, while also including similar protections. Anthropic says the funds will go toward advancing safety research, expanding compute, and scaling its products.
